mnemonics.title: WNOHANG记忆
mnemonics.content: WNOHANG = Wait NO HANG (不挂起) - Don't wait, return immediately if no child exited

mnemonics.title: WIFEXITED记忆
mnemonics.content: WIFEXITED = Wait If EXITED - Child terminated normally (exit() called)

mnemonics.title: WIFSIGNALED记忆
mnemonics.content: WIFSIGNALED = Wait If SIGNALED - Child killed by uncaught signal

mnemonics.title: WEXITSTATUS记忆
mnemonics.content: WEXITSTATUS = Wait EXIT STATUS - Get the actual exit code after confirming normal exit

acronyms.expansion: Wait No Hang - Non-blocking option that returns immediately if no child has terminated
acronyms.usage: Used in polling loops to check child status without blocking

acronyms.expansion: Wait Untraced - Report status of stopped child processes
acronyms.usage: Used for job control to track stopped processes

acronyms.expansion: Wait If Exited - Macro to check normal termination
acronyms.usage: Always check before using WEXITSTATUS to extract exit code

acronyms.expansion: Wait Term Signal - Get signal number that terminated process
acronyms.usage: Used when WIFSIGNALED returns true to identify the killing signal

visualTips.visualization: Think of waitpid(pid, &status, options) as: WHO (pid) is waiting for WHAT (status) with HOW (options)
Number line: -1=any group, -2=group2, 0=my group, +N=specific child N, -N=group N
32-bit status: [16 bits unused][8 bits exit code][8 bits signal info] - macros extract specific parts

analogies.analogy: Like a person who has died but the death certificate hasn't been filed - they occupy space until paperwork (waitpid) is done
wait() is like waiting for ANY bus, while waitpid() is like waiting for a SPECIFIC bus number (or using WNOHANG to just check if your bus came)
Checking your mailbox without waiting - you look and return immediately if empty, rather than waiting for mailman
